insight: New Zealand's performance in the 2026 World Cup hydration breaks reveals some interesting trends. When they were pressing before a break, they tended to lose momentum, with their average change in momentum being -0.200 per minute, and they were unable to regain it after the break. However, when they weren't pressing before a break, they actually gained momentum, with an average change of +0.100 per minute, suggesting that the break allowed them to regroup and come back stronger. Overall, the data suggests that hydration breaks can be a double-edged sword for New Zealand, potentially disrupting their rhythm when they're on the front foot, but also providing an opportunity to recover and refocus when they're not.
